text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. One effective method is to set SMART goals, which are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. This helps team members understand exactly what they are working towards. Breaking the project into milestones is also helpful, as small, achievable targets allow you to track progress and stay motivated. Additionally, defining the key deliverables—the final products or results—ensures everyone knows what to produce. Finally, stakeholder alignment is crucial. You need to discuss goals with everyone involved, such as clients or managers, to confirm their expectations match yours. Without this agreement, misunderstandings can occur later.
